title: Carla Hall's Spiced Fried Chicken

brine: 1/4 cup kosher salt (reserve 1 teaspoon more for the dredge)
frying: Neutral cooking oil, such as canola, enough for about 1/2 inch depth in skillet
Additional kosher salt for finishing, if desired

steps: Whisk together the water, sugar, 1/4 cup of the kosher salt, rosemary sprigs, thyme sprigs, bay leaves, and smashed garlic in a large bowl until the sugar dissolves. Submerge the chicken thighs in the brine and refrigerate at least 1 hour or up to 6 hours.
Combine the flour, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, ground thyme, ground rosemary, black pepper, and the remaining 1 teaspoon kosher salt in a large ziplock plastic bag.

Remove the chicken from the brine and thoroughly pat dry. Working in batches, add the chicken to the flour mixture and shake until well-coated. Transfer to a plate and repeat with the remaining chicken until all pieces are coated.
Tip from Carla: After flouring the chicken, let it sit for a few minutes. People often skip this step, but it's how you get a nice crunchy crust that doesn't fall off.
Pour oil into a cast-iron skillet (or other heavy skillet) to a depth of about 1/2 inch. Heat the oil over medium-high until a thermometer registers 325 degrees F.
Working in batches, carefully place the chicken in the hot oil, skin side down. Do not overlap pieces or overcrowd the skillet. Cook until browned on the bottom, 6 to 8 minutes.
Flip each piece and cook until golden brown all over and a thermometer inserted into the thickest portion of the chicken registers at least 165 degrees F, 6 to 8 minutes more.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and place on crumpled paper towels to drain. Repeat the frying process with the remaining chicken, replenishing and reheating the oil between batches.
Sprinkle with kosher salt, if desired, and serve. Serves 4. Active time: 1 hour. Total time: 2 hours.
